decisionPanels.title: 🎙️ Audio +45% YoY — MMR audio schema is urgent, Guardian Studios must treat it as first-class
decisionPanels.summary: Backlight's 2026 Media Stats Report confirms audio assets grew 45% year-on-year across its platform — the fastest-growing asset type after vertical video. The Guardian launched its flagship video podcast in February and just formed Guardian Studios with a mandate for 'personality-led journalism.' The infrastructure timing is now critical: Leah Green (head, Guardian Studios, ex-Channel 4) explicitly cited podcasts as part of the creative hub's output. MMR's LucidLink + iconik stack must handle audio-first asset management at scale, not as an afterthought. 903 million total assets and 324 PB tracked across Backlight's platform.
actions: Audit MMR iconik metadata schema for audio assets: does it support podcast-specific fields (episode number, series, language, transcript attachment)?
Ensure Project Launcher covers audio production workflows — not just video. Guardian Studios will need audio collections in iconik from day one.
Raise with Leah Green's team: what audio asset types does Guardian Studios plan to produce? Match MMR's iconik metadata schema to their needs before go-live.

decisionPanels.title: ☁️ LucidLink Frame.io integration + Connect GA — two infrastructure upgrades now at Q1 2026
decisionPanels.summary: Two LucidLink capabilities have reached or are at their GA target window simultaneously. LucidLink Connect (GA confirmed March 5, multiple sources) enables existing AWS S3 buckets to stream into LucidLink Filespaces without data migration. And the LucidLink + Frame.io integration — announced October 2025 at Adobe MAX with a Q1 2026 GA target — is now due. The integration means media uploaded through Frame.io's Camera-to-Cloud appears instantly in LucidLink, letting editors cut directly in Premiere Pro or After Effects while producers keep approvals in Frame.io. Included with LucidLink Business and Enterprise plans. If Guardian multimedia producers use Frame.io for client reviews or Camera-to-Cloud ingestion on location, this is an immediate workflow upgrade.
actions: Confirm with LucidLink account team: has Frame.io integration reached GA? If yes, evaluate for Guardian multimedia on-location workflows (Camera-to-Cloud → LucidLink → Premiere).
Ask Guardian infrastructure/cloud team: does Guardian have existing media archives on AWS S3? If yes, LucidLink Connect (Enterprise) can activate them without migration.
LucidLink just won a Television Academy Emmy for transforming TV workflows — worth including in any internal MMR business case.

decisionPanels.title: 🎬 AWS Elemental Inference + UK video ads £9.3bn — live vertical publishing is an infrastructure question NOW
decisionPanels.summary: Two signals that together define the stakes. AWS Elemental Inference delivers AI-powered real-time 9:16 vertical video from live broadcasts at 6-10 second latency — Fox Sports and NBCUniversal already live. Guardian is covering a major active conflict (US-Israel-Iran war, live since March) and publishing multiple video explainers per week. The question: can Guardian publish live 9:16 clips *during* breaking news events with no post-production step? Second: IAB UK confirms UK digital video ad investment hit £9.3 billion in 2025, +20% YoY, 24% of all UK digital ad spend. The commercial case for Guardian video infrastructure investment is irrefutable — the only constraint is infrastructure readiness.
actions: Evaluate AWS Elemental Inference for Guardian's live news vertical publishing — particularly for the Iran-US-Israel conflict coverage and future live events.
Ensure MMR Project Launcher metadata templates treat vertical (9:16) as a first-class asset type with its own delivery destination fields.
Brief Guardian Studios on IAB UK data: video is 24% of UK digital ad spend — this is the commercial argument for investing in Guardian Studios infrastructure now.

decisionPanels.title: 📱 Journalist-as-creator model is here — NYT Watch Tab, Nieman Lab prediction confirmed, TikTok watch-time favours real journalists
decisionPanels.summary: Three signals converging today. Nieman Lab (Dec 2025): 'The journalist who reports on a story increasingly becomes the person telling it on vertical platforms through their own account. Social teams shift from being the on-camera talent to being editorial support units.' NYT launched a curated vertical video Watch Tab in October 2025; Washington Post is following. Leah Green's Guardian Studios mandate is explicitly 'personality-led journalism' and building 'a new generation of Guardian fans' through content 'people seek out week by week.' And TikTok's 2026 algorithm now prioritises watch time over views, rewards niche-specific content, and explicitly favours authentic human creators over AI-generated videos. This is the editorial model and the distribution model aligning: Guardian journalists as on-screen talent is both a creative and algorithmic strategy.
actions: Ask Guardian Studios: is there a plan for a Guardian vertical video Watch feed? NYT and WaPo are ahead — timing matters for audience development.
MMR's Project Launcher should have a 'journalist-as-creator' publish flow: single asset → approve → publish to journalist's branded vertical feed.

TikTok 2026: qualified views (>5 seconds) matter more than raw views. Guardian's explainer format (Iran war, economic impact videos) is structurally well-suited — ensure social team knows the new benchmark.

items.title: iconik changelog March 5, 2026: My Shares filtering (All/Shared with Me/Shared by Me) + favourite saved searches — still latest confirmed update
iconik interface overhaul live from 4 March 2026: new nav, grey background, admin dropdown, Upload button removed — design system continues throughout 2026 (light/dark mode coming)
Backlight 2026 Iconik Media Stats Report — video = 64% of storage, audio +45% YoY, vertical publishing +120%, AI used 11.4M times, 903M assets, 324 PB total

LucidLink + Frame.io integration: Q1 2026 GA target — Camera-to-Cloud media appears instantly in LucidLink Filespace; editors cut directly in Premiere/After Effects; included with Business + Enterprise plans

NAB Show 2026 (April 18-22): Backlight booth N2829 — 'single platform to manage, create and publish media at unbelievable speed'; key vendor announcements expected
Guardian Studios confirmed: Leah Green (ex-Channel 4 — Dispatches, Unreported World, Untold) as head — video-first, personality-led journalism mandate; 'new generation of Guardian fans'
Guardian SPUR coalition (BBC, FT, Sky News, Telegraph): technical standards for AI use of publisher content — UK AI copyright delay strengthens SPUR's early-mover position
Guardian active conflict video output: US-Israel-Iran war (started March 2026) driving high-frequency explainers, timelines, on-the-ground reporter video — MMR workflows under live stress test
Guardian Media Group strategy: 'more global, more digital, more reader-funded' — CEO Anna Bateson; Guardian Studios is the video execution arm of this strategy
NYT Watch Tab (Oct 2025) + Washington Post following: curated vertical video feed with branded content planned early 2026 — Guardian conspicuously absent from this trend
TikTok 2026 algorithm: watch time over views, rewards niche content, favours authentic human creators over AI-generated — 'qualified views' (>5s) gate expansion past 200-view jail
AWS Elemental Inference: AI real-time vertical video (9:16) from live feeds at 6-10s latency — Fox Sports + NBCUniversal live; direct infrastructure option for live breaking news publishing
IAB UK: UK digital video ad spend £9.3bn in 2025, +20% YoY — now 24% of all UK digital ad spend; CTV = 34%; 57% of marketers expect higher video budgets in 2026
Nieman Lab: journalist-as-creator model arriving in 2026 — reporters become on-screen talent on vertical platforms; social teams become editorial support units (Dec 2025)
Google Flow March 2026 redesign: unified AI video workspace combining generation, editing, and animation — absorbs Whisk + ImageFX; all-in-one AI video platform for newsroom AI tooling watch
BBC Sport digital-first at Milan Cortina 2026: social/online platforms as publish priority, not afterthought — benchmark workflow for Guardian Studios
BBC: AI-generated content surge — 'pipeline onto social media almost fully automated'; authentic Guardian journalism becomes editorial competitive advantage vs AI content flood

changes.summary: Tonight's most important signal: three forces are converging around the journalist-as-creator model. Nieman Lab predicted it, NYT's Watch Tab proved it, and Guardian Studios' mandate under Leah Green is explicitly it. TikTok's 2026 algorithm update (watch time > views, authentic human creators > AI content) is the distribution validation. The workflow question for MMR: does Project Launcher need a 'journalist-to-vertical-platform' publish flow? Second priority: LucidLink + Frame.io integration hit its Q1 2026 GA target — confirm with LucidLink account team whether it's live now. Third: no new iconik changelog since March 5 confirmed. Guardian is currently under live stress-test with high-frequency Iran war video output — MMR workflows are being exercised in real-time.
